qa_pairs.question: What permissions must parent directories have for a user to access a shared subdirectory on a national cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: For a user to access a shared subdirectory, all parent directories in the path leading to it must have execute permission granted to that user. Public read permission on these parent directories is not required.

qa_pairs.question: How can execute permission be granted on a project directory (`/projects/def-<PI>`) to facilitate data sharing with individual users on a national cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Execute permission on a project directory (`/projects/def-<PI>`) can be granted either for everyone using the command `chmod o+x /project/def-<PI>/` or individually for specific users one-by-one using `setfacl -m u:username:X /project/def-<PI>/`.
